findings.title: Excessive nesting: 15 levels in writePreamble
findings.description:
15 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**
In function: `writePreamble`

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 15 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=3, p95=4, mean=1.2, n=269
findings.suggested_fix:
Severely nested code. Apply multiple techniques:
1. Guard clauses: `if (!condition) return;`
2. Extract Method: pull nested blocks into functions
3. Replace conditionals with polymorphism
4. Use functional patterns (map/filter instead of nested loops)
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.

findings.title: String concatenation in loop
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Variable 'comp' concatenated 2 times inside loop (started line 404).

**Performance:** O(n²) time complexity. Each concatenation creates a new string and copies all previous characters.

For 1000 iterations, this copies ~500,000 characters instead of 1000.
findings.suggested_fix:
Use strings.Builder:
```go
var sb strings.Builder
for _, item := range items {
sb.WriteString(item)
}
result := sb.String()
```
findings.why_it_matters: String concatenation in loops creates O(n²) time complexity due to immutable string copying.

findings.title: Complexity Outlier: execute
findings.description: Function 'execute' is a statistical complexity outlier (z-score: 7.5, complexity: 38).
findings.suggested_fix: Review and refactor - consider breaking into smaller functions
findings.why_it_matters: Complexity outliers indicate functions that need review and decomposition

findings.title: Excessive nesting: 8 levels in getCompletions
findings.description:
8 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**
In function: `getCompletions`
High complexity: 57 (nesting compounds this)
Existing helpers that could reduce nesting: checkIfFlagCompletion, completeRequireFlags, helpOrVersionFlagPresent

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 8 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=3, p95=4, mean=1.2, n=269
findings.suggested_fix:
This function already has helpers like `checkIfFlagCompletion`. Consider:
1. Extract more nested blocks into similar helpers
2. Use guard clauses (early returns) to reduce nesting
3. Replace nested ifs with switch/match
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.

findings.title: Long method: InitDefaultCompletionCmd (182 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'InitDefaultCompletionCmd' has 182 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Orchestrator pattern: calls 14 functions (reduced severity)
findings.suggested_fix:
This appears to be an orchestrator function (coordinates many calls).
If it must remain long, ensure it:
1. Has clear section comments
2. Handles errors at each step
3. Has a clear flow (consider a state machine for complex flows)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.
